observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to reclassify all PPE costing below ₱50,000 and
draw a journal entry voucher (JEV) to make the necessary adjusting
entries to effect the derecognition thereof from the books of the LGU; and
b. GSO and the Municipal Accountant to use the prescribed ledgers,
reports and registries for semi-expendable properties.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of local
roads and prepare the RPCLRN using the prescribed format guided by the
instructions in Annex C of the aforesaid COA Circular;
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Inventory Committee and the
Municipal Accountant with a complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects;
c. Require the Municipal Engineer to submit an updated RPCLRN ensuring
that private roads are not included in the report;
d. Require the GSO, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the inventory of local roads with the recorded road networks in the
books of accounts to account for the reported discrepancy;
e. Require the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain
the LRN Property Card and Ledger Card, respectively; and
f. Require the Municipal Accountant to provide proper and complete
disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management prepare and issue Acknowledgment
Receipts for In-Kind Donations for DRRM or his/her authorized
representative for the receipt of in-kind donations in the future.
We also recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that proper recognition
and disclosure of donations in-kind are made in the Financial
Statements;
b. Instruct the GSO to prepare and maintain Registry of Donated Relief
Goods for DRRM for each kind of relief goods using the prescribed
format; and
c. Direct the MSWDO to prepare monthly reports on the receipt and
distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations. Separate reports
shall be prepared for relief goods and PPE based on the Registry of
Donated Relief Goods for DRRM and Property Cards, respectively.
The report shall be submitted to the OCD, copy furnish the COA
Auditor, on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month until all
donations are fully consumed/utilized.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to take up the tilapia
broodstock breeder as Breeding Stocks;
b. Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to maintain records relative to the
progress of the tilapia breeding activities of the LGU for proper
monitoring; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to ensure the
preparation and maintenance of Work, Other Animals and Breeding
Stocks Ledger Card and Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks
Property Card, respectively.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the cause of
variances appearing in the PPELCs and SLCs against the schedules
supporting the financial statements and make the necessary adjustments;
and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all PPELCs and SLCs
tally with the data presented in the financial statements in order to
provide a reliable, detailed and organized tracking system for
acquisitions, issuances, and balances of PPE and supplies.

We recommended that Management has Management: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to: • Ensure that replenishment of PCF by the custodian be made when the total disbursements reach at least 75 percent and analyze if the current amount set-up for PCF is not enough to cover the requirements of the LGU and recommend the possible increase of the fund; • Check the correctness and completeness of each PCV before giving out the fund including checks and the assignment of number series of PCVs; • Ensure that appropriate box was checked before accepting the liquidation documents of accountable officers and that the payee signed 'Box D'; • Appropriately record all transactions in the cashbook and extend the fund balance after every entry to facilitate quick and accurate reconciliation of cash accounts, allowing for efficient monitoring of financial transactions; and • Provide certification in the cashbook every month-end to ensure that the recorded cash balance in the cashbook is accurate and reflect the actual funds available. b. Instruct the PCF custodian to: • Release cash advances only to the person who requested the fund from the PCF, unless an authorization letter from the requestor is handed by their representative; • Check the correctness and completeness of each PCV before giving out the fund requested including checks and the assignment of number series of the PCVs; • Ensure that appropriate boxes in PCVs are checked before accepting the liquidation documents of accountable officers and that the payee has signed 'Box D'; • Appropriately record all transactions in the cashbook and extend the fund balance after every entry to facilitate quick and accurate reconciliation of cash accounts, allowing for efficient monitoring of financial transactions; and • Provide certification in the cashbook every month-end to ensure that the recorded cash balance in the cashbook is accurate and reflect the actual funds available.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation. The Municipal Treasurer ensures that replenishment of PCF by the custodian are made when the total disbursements reach at least 75 percent. Regular assessment and monitoring of the cash disbursement process were observed. Verified that the replenishment made by the Petty Cash custodian of the fund were within the proper threshold. Also, during the cash count (August 29, 2024) on the cash accounts of the Accountable Officers conducted by the Audit Team in the Municipal Treasury Office, including the Petty cash Custodian, it was verified that the replenishments made by the PCF Custodian were already in accordance with the COA Circular No. 97-002. Further, according to the Municipal Treasurer, an increase of the PCF is not yet necessary based on the internal assessment that they made. They assured the Audit Team that they will continue to regularly review and monitor their PCF. Should they observe any insufficiency or delays in the processing of cash disbursements, they will promptly propose to Management a possible increase in the PCF. Verified that the Petty Cash custodian strictly releases cash advances only to the person who actually requested the fund. The Municipal requested the PCF custodian to strictly releases cash advances only to the person who requested the fund including checks and the assignment of number series of PCVs. Verified that the PCF custodian strictly verifies the correctness and completeness of each PCV before giving out the fund requested including the assignment of number series of PCVs. Verified that the PCF custodian ensures that cashbook maintained by the PCF custodian and proper recording of transactions was observed. The Audit Team examined the cashbook maintained by the PCF custodian and verified that proper recording of transactions was observed. Verified that the cashbook are updated every month-end with certification ensuring that the recorded cash balance in the cash book is accurate and reflect the actual funds available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the GSO to: • Prepare the SSMI using the prescribed format on a weekly basis duly supported with complete RIS and furnish the Accounting Office a copy as basis for recording the expense; • Conduct physical count, in coordination with the Inventory Committee, semestrally and prepare the report and submit the same to the Audit Team within the reglementary period; b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Ensure that the quantity issued for every supply and material indicated in the SSMI is completely supported with RIS before preparing a JEV to record the complete expenditures; • Prepare a JEV to reclassify the affected supplies and materials to their appropriate inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation. The Supply Officer started using the prescribed format of SSMI with complete RIS. The GSO, in coordination with the Inventory Committee, conducted the semestral physical count of inventories and submitted the related report to COA. The Municipal Accountant ensures that the SSMIs are already supported by complete RIS. The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 100-2024-04-0120 dated April 30, 2024 to reclassify the supplies and materials to their appropriate books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to effect the reclassification of misclassified PPE accounts to the appropriate accounts including their corresponding Accumulated Depreciation.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation. The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV Nos. 100-2024-04-0121 and 200-2024-04-008 both dated April 30, 2024 to reclassify misclassified PPE accounts to their appropriate accounts, including the necessary adjustments on their corresponding Accumulated Depreciation adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized balance of ₱33,000.00 to the source agency and the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the same in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation. The Municipal Treasurer returned the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for capital outlay previously transferred to the Special Trust Fund to the source agency through Check No. 0051693730 dated April 29, 2024. The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 300-2024-04-007 dated April 25, 2024 and recorded the same in the books of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the PSDS to sign and certify the PRs, CAFOAs and DVs for transactions charged against the SEF.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation. The Municipal instructed the PSDS in various CAFOAs, PRs and DVs for transactions charged to SEF to sign and certify. Verification of documents transmitted to the Audit Team revealed that CAFOAs, PRs and DVs charged to SEF were already signed by the PSDS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Direct the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman, to ensure that the list of all on-going government PPAs, as well as those awarded and those that are to be implemented during the year which have been awarded and the corresponding monthly monitoring report are prepared and submitted within the prescribed period to the Audit Team; b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to notify the SA and ATL on the posting of signboards for infrastructure and non-infrastructure PPAs; and c. Require the Municipal Engineer to ensure that contractors erect the signboards with full disclosure of information at the project site to promote good governance through transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation. Monitoring reports for all ongoing government PPAs, as well as those awarded for PPAs were prepared and submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipal Engineer has been notifying the SA and ATL regarding the posting of signboards for projects of the LGU. On-site inspection by the Audit Team revealed that signboards were erected in the project sites of LGU projects and that these signboards already contained information relative to the status of the concerned projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for capital outlay previously transferred to the Special Trust Fund to the General Fund. We also recommended that Management ensure that all unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for capital outlay shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefor are completed and require the MDRRMO to submit within the prescribed period the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization to the Audit Team for the timely review of the fund utilization and evaluation of the projects and activities implemented.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation. A copy of DV No. 300-2024-04-009 and Check No. 0051693737 dated April 30, 2024 was furnished to the Audit Team and verified that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for capital outlay was transferred and recorded in the books. The Municipal Accountant monitors the unexpended balances and ensures that any savings will be made available for disaster risk reduction and management activities. The MDRRMO, in coordination with the Accounting Office, has prepared and submitted Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to: a. Make the necessary inspection of concerned MVs and act immediately for the essential repairs needed; b. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to secure from the donor of the two MVs a copy of the LTO Certificate of Registration and LTO Official Receipt in order to process the renewal of the donated motor vehicles and obtain ensure that future acceptance of donated MVs are accompanied with the necessary documents needed for registration; and c. Prioritize and ensure the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles owned by the LGU in CY 2024 and years thereafter.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation in part. Regular monitoring of inspections and motor vehicle repairs commenced in May 2024, ensuring that all serviceable motor vehicles have been registered beginning in June 2024. The GSO followed up with the donor to secure copies of the LTO Certificate of Registration and Official Receipt for the two donated motor vehicles, but has yet to receive a response. The GSO continues to follow-up and plans to send a formal request letter to demand the necessary documents. Verification revealed that regular inspections and monitoring of motor vehicle repairs had commenced in May 2024, ensuring that all vehicles were renewed on time.
observations.reason: Follow-up with the donor for the documents for the two donated motor vehicles is ongoing; response not yet received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the MSWDO to submit to the audit team copy of the pictures of the beneficiaries during actual receipt of the assistance/stipend in compliance with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09, series of 2020 and for completeness of supporting documents in the disbursement of public funds.
observations.managementAction: Not all pictures have been submitted because according to the MSWDO some files have been corrupted.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that MSWDO take the necessary actions to facilitate the refund of the subsidy by the ineligible beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: MSWDO have sent demand letters.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Environmental Officer (MENRO) to formulate and immediately implement a long term solution leading to a self-reliant LGU, as mandated by the Local Government Code and RA No. 9003, to address its solid wastes for the benefit of the local constituents.
observations.managementAction: In the absence of their own sanitary landfill, they are implementing alternative measures and strategies to address the solid waste problem of the Municipality such as the “No segregation, no collection policy” and “No Plastic Ordinance”. The MRF in Barangay Quibaol is undergoing rehabilitation for the proposed eco- tourism park. Likewise, the 32 barangays are in the process of identifying lots for their own MRFs. They are also conducting a feasibility study to undertake a partnership with a private company for the installation of a facility which will convert wastes to energy. Management assured the Audit Team of their eagerness to implement a long-term solution leading to a self-reliant LGU and are looking on every way possible.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 14, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to re-
assess the real property tax payment of Globe Telecom, for the period 2015 up
to present, in relation to the recognition of contingent liability under IPSAS 19.
If found probable and can be measured reliably, instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to recognize liabilities in the
general fund books for all payments made by Globe Telecom; otherwise,
prepare a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements to disclose all
payments made by the company which is under protest and prepare adjusting
journal entries to correct the initial entry made in the Trust Fund books.

We recommended that Management:
➢ Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting
journal entries in the books of accounts to correct the entry made in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009, dated December 1, 2015;
➢ Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the succeeding receipt of
share from the Lotto Charity Fund as income using the Share from
PCSO account; and
➢ Utilize the funds in the conduct of charity programs and activities which
are consistent with the PCSO line-up of services under the Lotto Charity
Fund to become more effective partner of the national government in the
extension of services to the marginal sector in the community.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue to adopt the following measures:

Update and post notice of delinquency for property taxes at the main entrance of the Municipal Building and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay where these properties located.

Seek assistance from the concerned barangays for the issuance of Notice of Delinquency and form a committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.

Impose the 25% surcharge on rentals not paid on time plus interest of 2% per month on unpaid rentals, revocation of lease contract and be declared vacant and advertise for lease in accordance with the Municipal Ordinance No. 18, S-2014, the Market Code of the Municipality of Lingayen. In addition, direct the Market Supervisor to strictly monitor the implementation of the market policies to improve its collection efficiency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to adhere strictly with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM on the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the Development Fund.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Accounting Department to establish the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year and stop reversing the balance at the end of the year for fair presentation in the financial statements;
Recognize in the books the amount of collectibles from real property taxes both for General Fund and Special Education Fund in accordance with Section 20 of MNGAS to reflect the actual collectibles from real property taxes in the financial statements of the Municipality; and
Instruct the Treasury Office to facilitate the updating of the database in order to generate a timely and relevant report for recording in the books.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to observe the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-001 in the preparation and utilization of 20% Development Fund; and
Stop utilizing the development fund for purposes other than intended in accordance with the afore-cited laws, rules and regulations and furnish the office of the DILG and DBM Regional Office a copy of the approved AIP pursuant to the afore-cited Joint Memorandum Circular.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging expenditures against the LDRRMF which are not directly supportive of disaster risk management programs and activities. Further, we recommended that Management allocate a separate budget under the General Fund for the MDRRMO pursuant to the above-cited Circular. Likewise, advise the Municipal Budget Officer to obligate the disbursements relative to the utilization of LDRRMF in accordance with the approved LDRRMFI, the nature of the expenditure must be considered to ensure the attainment of the purpose intended.

We recommended that Management formulate and immediately implement a long term solution leading to a self-reliant LGU, as mandated by the Local Government Code and Republic Act 9003, to address its solid wastes for the benefit of the local constituents.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of its own resources to avoid incurring additional expenses and undertake all procurement through competitive bidding, except as provided in Rule XVI of the IRR of RA 9184.
